name: Paprika Pork Tenderloin Sandwiches
recipeInstructions:
Cut the pork tenderloin crosswise into 4 pieces.
1. In a shallow bowl, combine flour and paprika; mix well. Place milk in a second shallow dish. In a third shallow bowl, combine bread crumbs, salt, onion powder, and pepper; mix well.
2. To flatten each piece of pork, place the cut side up between 2 sheets of plastic wrap or waxed paper. Working from the center, gently pound the pork with a flat side of a mallet or rolling pin until about 1/4 inch thick. Remove the wrap. Repeat with remaining pork pieces.
3. Dip each pork piece in flour mixture to coat. Dip in milk; then dip in bread crumb mixture to coat.
4. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until hot. Add pork tenderloin pieces and cook 5 to 6 minutes, or until the pork is no longer pink in the center, turning once. Place 2 pieces of pork tenderloin in each bun.
